title: EU Issues Record Fine to AliExpress Over Unsafe Goods
subtitle: The retail giant faces a €550 million penalty for failing to protect European consumers from dangerous and illegal products.

summary.en: The European Union has fined AliExpress a record €550 million for allowing the sale of illegal, unsafe, and fake products. Regulators found that the platform failed to properly check items like dangerous toys and cosmetics. AliExpress has rejected the fine as unfair and plans to appeal the decision. This penalty is the largest ever issued under the Digital Services Act, which sets strict rules for online safety. The company must now present a plan to fix these issues by October 20.
summary.zh: 歐盟對全球速賣通開出五億五千萬歐元的破紀錄罰單，理由是該平台允許非法、不安全及仿冒產品販售。監管機構發現，該平台未能妥善審核危險玩具與化妝品等商品。全球速賣通認為罰款不公並計畫提出上訴。這是「數位服務法」實施以來金額最高的罰單，該法規為網路安全制定了嚴格規範。該公司目前必須在十月二十日前提出修正這些問題的計畫。

paragraphs.en: The European Union has issued a record-breaking fine of €550 million to the Chinese online retail giant AliExpress. According to the European Commission, the company failed to stop the sale of illegal and harmful products on its platform, including unsafe toys, fake clothing, and dangerous cosmetics. This penalty is the largest ever imposed under the Digital Services Act (DSA), a set of laws designed to make the internet safer by requiring large tech companies to identify and remove illegal content.
paragraphs.zh: 歐盟對中國線上零售巨頭全球速賣通（AliExpress）開出了五億五千萬歐元的破紀錄罰單。根據歐盟執委會（European Commission）指出，該公司未能阻止非法與有害產品在其平台上架，其中包括不安全的玩具、仿冒服飾以及危險化妝品。這項罰款是根據「數位服務法」（Digital Services Act，簡稱 DSA）所處以金額最高的一次裁罰；該法規旨在透過要求大型科技公司識別並移除違法內容，進而提升網路環境的安全性。

paragraphs.en: European Commission official Henna Virkkunen stated that the spread of these harmful items is not an unavoidable cost of shopping online. Instead, she described it as a failure by AliExpress to follow its legal obligations. The decision follows a two-year investigation which found that the company’s systems for detecting dangerous goods did not work properly. In some cases, illegal products remained on the website for several weeks even after they were identified. Furthermore, the investigation revealed that AliExpress did not properly punish traders who sold these illegal items, allowing them to continue their activities.
paragraphs.zh: 歐盟執委會官員薇庫寧（Henna Virkkunen）表示，這些有害商品的散布並非線上購物不可避免的代價。相反地，她將此描述為全球速賣通未能履行其法律義務的失職。這項裁決源於一項為期兩年的調查，結果發現該公司用於偵測危險商品的系統運作並不完善。在某些案例中，即便非法產品已被識別，它們仍能在網站上停留數週之久。此外，調查還揭露全球速賣通並未對販售這些違禁品的商家進行適當懲處，導致這些商家得以持續其違規行為。

paragraphs.en: One of the main issues highlighted by the European Commission was a lack of staff. Reports from The Guardian and Ars Technica noted that the company did not have enough employees to review products effectively. Some moderators were reportedly given only "tens of seconds" to decide if a product met European safety standards. Because of this, many dangerous items were not blocked. Additionally, the commission found that the platform’s recommendation systems sometimes promoted these illegal goods to customers, which helped the items reach more people.
paragraphs.zh: 歐盟執委會所強調的主要問題之一是人力不足。根據《衛報》（The Guardian）與 Ars Technica 的報導指出，該公司沒有足夠的員工來有效審核產品。據傳，部分審核人員僅有「數十秒」的時間來判斷一項產品是否符合歐洲安全標準。正因如此，許多危險商品未能被攔截。此外，執委會發現該平台的推薦系統有時會向消費者推廣這些非法商品，進而協助這些商品觸及更多受眾。

paragraphs.en: AliExpress, which is owned by the Chinese tech conglomerate Alibaba, has 193 million users in Europe. This makes it a larger platform in the region than competitors like Shein or Temu. Despite the size of the fine, it represents less than 1% of the €122 billion in revenue that Alibaba earned last year. Under the Digital Services Act, the European Union has the power to fine companies up to 6% of their global revenue, meaning the potential penalty could have been much higher.
paragraphs.zh: 隸屬於中國科技集團阿里巴巴（Alibaba）旗下的全球速賣通，在歐洲擁有 1.93 億名用戶。這使其在該地區的規模大於希音（Shein）或拼多多海外版（Temu）等競爭對手。儘管罰款金額龐大，但這僅占阿里巴巴去年一千兩百二十億歐元營收的不到百分之一。根據「數位服務法」，歐盟有權對企業處以最高達其全球營收百分之六的罰款，這意味著潛在的罰金本可能更高。

paragraphs.en: In response to the news, AliExpress expressed surprise and disagreement with the decision. The company stated that the fine was "disproportionate"—meaning it was too large for the situation—and ignored the work it has already done to improve safety. In a statement, AliExpress claimed it invests significant resources into risk assessment and consumer protection. The company confirmed that it plans to appeal the decision, arguing that it has made proactive improvements to its systems.
paragraphs.zh: 針對此消息，全球速賣通對該裁決表示驚訝並持反對意見。該公司聲稱這筆罰款「不成比例」，意即對於當前情況而言過於沉重，且忽略了該公司為提升安全性所做的努力。全球速賣通在聲明中表示，公司投入了大量資源於風險評估與消費者保護。該公司並證實計畫對此裁決提出上訴，主張其已主動對系統進行改善。

paragraphs.en: This is not the first time the European Union has used the Digital Services Act to target large online platforms. Earlier this year, the retailer Temu was fined €200 million for similar issues regarding the sale of dangerous baby toys and other illegal products. Last year, the social media platform X was also fined €120 million for issues related to deceptive verification badges and a lack of transparency. These actions show that the European Union is becoming much stricter with how online companies manage the safety of their users.
paragraphs.zh: 這並非歐盟首次運用「數位服務法」針對大型線上平台採取行動。今年稍早，零售商拼多多海外版因販售危險嬰兒玩具及其他非法產品等類似問題，遭罰兩億歐元。去年，社群媒體平台 X 也因涉及欺騙性驗證標章及缺乏透明度等問題，被處以一億兩千萬歐元的罰款。這些行動顯示，歐盟對於線上企業如何管理用戶安全正採取日益嚴格的態度。

paragraphs.en: As part of the ruling, AliExpress is required to pay the fine and must submit a detailed plan to the European Commission by October 20. This plan must explain exactly how the company will fix its safety breaches and prevent illegal goods from being sold in the future. Once the plan is submitted, regulators will review it to see if the changes are sufficient. If the company fails to meet these new standards, it could face further penalties in the future.
paragraphs.zh: 作為裁決的一部分，全球速賣通必須繳納罰款，並須在十月二十日前向歐盟執委會提交詳細的改善計畫。該計畫必須明確說明公司將如何修正其安全漏洞，並防止未來再有非法商品販售。一旦計畫提交，監管機構將進行審查，以評估變更是否足夠。若該公司未能達到這些新標準，未來可能面臨進一步的懲處。

paragraphs.en: For many Europeans, this issue is very relevant. According to Henna Virkkunen, one in five people in the European Union shops on platforms like AliExpress, Temu, or Shein at least once a month. Because so many people rely on these sites for their daily needs, the European Commission believes it is essential to hold these companies to a high standard of safety. The investigation into AliExpress serves as a clear warning to other global retail platforms that they must take responsibility for the products sold on their websites.
paragraphs.zh: 對許多歐洲人而言，此議題與生活息息相關。根據薇庫寧的說法，歐盟每五人中就有一人每月至少會在全球速賣通、拼多多海外版或希音等平台上購物一次。由於許多人依賴這些網站滿足日常需求，歐盟執委會認為，要求這些企業遵守高標準的安全規範至關重要。針對全球速賣通的調查，對其他全球零售平台發出了明確警告，要求它們必須為在其網站上販售的產品負起責任。

keyTerms.definitionEn: Prevented from being seen, accessed, or sold.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（街區），這裡作動詞用，指未能阻止非法商品上架。文中：Because of this, many dangerous items were not blocked.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To select someone or something as the object of an attack or criticism.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 鎖定（作為攻擊或批評的對象）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（目標），這裡作動詞用，指歐盟針對大型平台採取行動。文中：This is not the first time the European Union has used the Digital Services Act to target large online platforms.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To require someone to be responsible for something.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞（拿著），這裡與 responsibility 連用，指要求公司負起安全責任。文中：Because so many people rely on these sites for their daily needs, the European Commission believes it is essential to hold these companies to a high standard of safety.

sources.title: AliExpress fined record €550m by EU for allowing sale of illegal goods

sources.name: The Guardian World
sources.title: AliExpress fined record €550m by EU for failing to stop sale of illegal and fake goods

sources.name: Deutsche Welle (DW) Top
sources.title: AliExpress hit with record EU fine

sources.name: Ars Technica
sources.title: AliExpress hit with record $625M fine after failing to make EU-ordered fixes

quiz.question: According to the investigation, what was a specific operational failure that contributed to the presence of illegal products on AliExpress?

options.A: The company lacked the necessary software to track international shipping routes.
options.B: Staff members were given an insufficient amount of time to evaluate product safety standards.
options.C: The company intentionally removed safety filters to increase the speed of customer transactions.
options.D: AliExpress refused to share its database of sellers with the European Commission.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文章第三段明確指出，由於人力不足，審核人員有時僅有「數十秒」的時間來判斷商品是否符合歐洲安全標準。選項 A 文章未提及；選項 C 錯誤，因為文章指出是系統無法有效運作，而非故意移除過濾器；選項 D 文章未提及拒絕分享資料庫，僅提到調查發現其系統運作不當。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the European Union's approach to regulating large online platforms?

options.A: The EU intends to ban all non-European retail platforms to protect local businesses.
options.B: The EU believes that the current fines are too high and will likely reduce them in the future.
options.C: The EU is increasingly assertive in holding digital companies accountable for user safety.
options.D: The EU is primarily focused on the financial revenue of companies rather than consumer protection.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據文章第六段與第八段，歐盟不僅處罰了 AliExpress，還對 Temu 和 X 進行了罰款，且官員強調這是一個對其他平台發出的「明確警告」，顯示歐盟正變得越來越嚴格，要求大型平台為用戶安全負起責任。選項 A 過度推論，文中未提及禁令；選項 B 與文中提到的「更嚴格」趨勢相悖；選項 D 錯誤，因為文章強調的是安全標準，而非僅關注營收。

quiz.question: In the fifth paragraph, what does the word 'disproportionate' mean as used by AliExpress?

options.A: Excessive or unfair in size relative to the circumstances.
options.B: Calculated based on a specific percentage of global revenue.
options.C: Completely unexpected and lacking any legal justification.
options.D: An amount that has been reduced due to the company's cooperation.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。在第五段中，AliExpress 認為罰款是「disproportionate」，並在括號中解釋為「對該情況而言金額太大」，意指罰款與其過失程度或努力程度不成比例。選項 B 是事實描述，非該詞意涵；選項 C 語意過重，公司僅是認為金額不公，未否定法律依據；選項 D 與文意相反，公司認為罰款過高，而非因合作而減少。

quiz.question: Which of the following best summarizes the main point of the article?

options.A: AliExpress has become the most popular online shopping platform in Europe despite its many legal challenges.
options.B: The European Union is using the Digital Services Act to enforce safety standards on major online retailers.
options.C: The Chinese tech conglomerate Alibaba is planning to withdraw from the European market due to high fines.
options.D: Consumers in Europe are demanding that the government ban platforms like AliExpress and Temu for selling fake goods.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。本文的主旨圍繞在歐盟透過《數位服務法》(DSA) 對 AliExpress 等大型平台開罰，目的是為了要求這些公司改善平台安全並移除違法商品。選項 A 是背景資訊，非主旨；選項 C 錯誤，文中僅提到公司將提出上訴，未提及退出市場；選項 D 錯誤，文中提到很多人依賴這些平台，並未提到消費者要求全面禁止。
